auth_files.title: Auth Files Management
auth_files.description: Manage all CLI Proxy JSON auth files here (e.g. Qwen, Gemini, Vertex). Uploading a credential immediately enables the corresponding AI integration.
auth_files.empty_desc: Click the button above to upload the first file
auth_files.search_empty_desc: Try changing the filters or clearing the search box.
auth_files.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete file
auth_files.delete_all_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete all auth files? This operation cannot be undone!
auth_files.delete_filtered_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete all {{type}} auth files? This operation cannot be undone!
auth_files.delete_filtered_partial: {{type}} auth files deletion finished: {{success}} succeeded, {{failed}} failed
auth_files.delete_filtered_none: No deletable auth files under the current filter ({{type}})
auth_files.search_placeholder: Filter by name, type, or provider
auth_files.too_many_files_warning: Too many credentials. Showing all may cause performance issues, please use paged view.
auth_files.models_empty: No available models for this credential
auth_files.models_empty_desc: This credential may not be loaded by the server yet, or no models are bound to it.
auth_files.models_unsupported: This feature is not supported in the current version
auth_files.models_unsupported_desc: Please update CLI Proxy API to the latest version and try again
auth_files.batch_delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{count}} files?
auth_files.priority_hint: Integers only. Invalid values are ignored. Larger value means higher priority.
auth_files.excluded_models_placeholder: Comma or newline separated, e.g. model-a, gpt-5-*, *-preview
auth_files.excluded_models_hint: Saved as an array and normalized by trim/lowercase/dedup/sort.
auth_files.disable_cooling_hint: Supports booleans, numeric 0/non-0, and strings like true/false/1/0; unparseable values are ignored.
auth_files.prefix_proxy_invalid_json: This auth file is not a JSON object, so fields cannot be edited.
auth_files.fetch_models_desc: Fetch models from /model-definitions and batch generate aliases (default alias=same).
auth_files.model_alias_desc: Maintain Models name -> alias mapping by channel (for OAuth).
auth_files.prefix_proxy_desc: Only modifies prefix/proxy_url, everything else remains unchanged (by re-uploading file with same name).
auth_files.no_models_api: Server does not implement /auth-files/models or auth file does not support querying models.
auth_files.no_excluded_api: Server does not implement /oauth-excluded-models (or version is too old). Please update the server.
auth_files.no_alias_api: Server does not implement /oauth-model-alias. Please upgrade the server.
auth_files.one_model_per_line: One model per line; use * to disable all models
auth_files.not_valid_json: File is not valid JSON and cannot be edited.
auth_files.not_json_object: File is not a JSON object and cannot be edited.
auth_files.edit_channel_name_desc: Set a recognizable name for this OAuth channel so API key channel restrictions can target it.
auth_files.channel_name_hint: This name appears in the API key allowed-channels selector and must be globally unique.
auth_files.usage_stats_warning: Usage stats loading failed: File management is not affected, but success/failed stats will show 0.
auth_files.detail_tab_json_desc: View the raw JSON content of this auth file.
auth_files.detail_tab_models_desc: Query supported models for this credential to troubleshoot permissions and exclusions.
auth_files.trend_window_week_desc: Daily requests and weekly quota dimensions.
auth_files.trend_empty_desc: Quota snapshots start accumulating after this feature is enabled.
auth_files.trend_missing_auth_index: This auth file is missing auth_index.
auth_files.trend_no_quota_series: No quota trend samples for this window yet.
auth_files.leave_empty_proxy_id: Leave empty to remove proxy_id. proxy_url below is kept as fallback.
auth_files.save_note: Note: Saving will re-upload the file; recommended max size is {{size}}.
auth_files.server_no_excluded_api: Server does not support OAuth excluded models API (/oauth-excluded-models). Please upgrade.
auth_files.server_no_alias_api: Server does not support OAuth model alias API (/oauth-model-alias). Please upgrade.
auth_files.failed_get_model_owners: Failed to get model owner groups
auth_files.model_owner_group_unavailable: The selected owner group is no longer available.
auth_files.no_owner_group_models: This owner group has no models.
auth_files.download_sensitive_confirm: This downloads the full auth file and may include sensitive credentials. Continue?
auth_files.confirm_delete_filter: Are you sure you want to delete auth files under current filter ({{filter}})? This operation is irreversible.
auth_files.confirm_delete_file: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}? This operation is irreversible.
auth_files.virtual_hint: Virtual auth files are for runtime injection only: cannot View/Download/Edit/Delete.
auth_files.confirm_delete_all: Are you sure you want to delete all auth files? This operation cannot be undone.
auth_files.load_excluded_failed: Failed to load OAuth excluded models
auth_files.load_alias_failed: Failed to load OAuth model aliases
auth_files.subscription_started_at_hint: Set the subscription start time and billing cycle. The expiration date is calculated automatically; leave the start time empty to remove it.
auth_files.subscription_started_at_invalid: Please enter a valid subscription start time.
auth_files.group_overview_modal_desc: Review auth file counts, call totals, and average quota usage across channel groups.
auth_files.group_overview_avg_week_help: Based on current weekly quota snapshot
auth_files.group_overview_channels_desc: View call counts and 5h/weekly quota averages for each channel in the selected group.
auth_files.group_overview_empty: No channels to show for this group
auth_files.group_overview_no_quota: No aggregatable quota in current results

oauth_excluded.description: Per-provider model disablement is shown as cards; click a card to edit or delete. Wildcards * are supported and the scope follows the auth file filter.

image_generation.title: Image Models
image_generation.description: Check how to call `gpt-image-2` for text-to-image and image-to-image, and validate generation through the test modal.
image_generation.call_description: Use an API key from the API Keys page to call the image endpoints. The service automatically rotates available Codex OAuth channels.
image_generation.text_to_image_desc: Generate an image directly from a prompt. Best for creating assets from scratch.
image_generation.image_to_image_desc: Upload a reference image and describe the edit direction. Best for restyling, repainting, and transformations.
image_generation.param_edit_prompt_desc: Describe how the uploaded image should be changed.
image_generation.param_image_desc: Image file to edit, uploaded with multipart/form-data.
image_generation.param_images_desc: Image files to edit. Repeat the multipart/form-data `image` field, up to 5 images.
image_generation.param_quality_desc: Optional quality: low, medium, or high.
image_generation.param_n_desc: Number of images to generate. Recommended range: 1-4.
image_generation.param_response_format_desc: Use b64_json so request logs can preview the generated image directly.
image_generation.response_b64_desc: Base64 image content; can be joined into a data URL for preview.
image_generation.response_revised_prompt_desc: Revised prompt returned by upstream, when available.
image_generation.channels_description: When no usable codex OAuth auth is configured, the page shows an unavailable hint and disables testing.
image_generation.channels_empty: No channels are currently available for `gpt-image-2`.
image_generation.test_description: Open the test modal, enter a prompt, and run a live image generation check.
image_generation.prompt_placeholder: Enter a prompt, for example: an orange tabby cat sitting by a window, cinematic light, photorealistic
image_generation.test_hint: Test calls automatically rotate through currently available channels.
image_generation.idle_hint: Enter a prompt to start generating an image
image_generation.idle_hint_edits: Upload images and enter a prompt to start generating
image_generation.generating_subtitle: Keep this modal open. The latest image will replace this area when ready.
image_generation.test_empty_result: Upstream did not return an image result

identity_fingerprint.title: Identity Fingerprint
identity_fingerprint.description: Unify provider client identities sent upstream and reduce terminal-specific differences.
identity_fingerprint.codex_enabled_desc: When enabled, local terminal User-Agent, Version, Session_id, and related fingerprint headers are not passed through.
identity_fingerprint.basic_desc: These fields are sent upstream as the Codex client identity. Keep them consistent across terminals.
identity_fingerprint.user_agent_hint: The Codex client identity visible to upstream.
identity_fingerprint.version_hint: The Codex client version visible to upstream.
identity_fingerprint.session_desc: Controls how Session_id is generated. Random per request is recommended so multiple clients are not collapsed into one session.
identity_fingerprint.session_id_placeholder: Leave blank to let the server generate one
identity_fingerprint.session_id_hint: Only used by fixed mode. Fixing every session ID is not recommended for normal use.
identity_fingerprint.advanced_desc: Usually no change is needed. Configure these only when upstream needs extra headers.
identity_fingerprint.custom_headers_hint: Enter a JSON object. Authorization, User-Agent, Version, and other managed headers cannot be set here.
identity_fingerprint.notice_desc: The default template uses the current server-side Codex CLI fingerprint. Turning this on or off affects following requests immediately; no service restart is required.
identity_fingerprint.preview_desc: After saving and enabling, upstream mainly sees these unified values.
identity_fingerprint.claude_enabled_desc: When enabled, Anthropic OAuth requests receive a full Claude Code-style header, billing, session, and metadata fingerprint.
identity_fingerprint.claude_desc: Edit the seven Claude Code identity dimensions sent to Anthropic OAuth upstream requests.
identity_fingerprint.claude_cli_version_hint: Also drives the default User-Agent and billing header version.
identity_fingerprint.claude_user_agent_hint: User-Agent sent to Anthropic OAuth upstream requests.
identity_fingerprint.claude_stainless_desc: These values mirror the @anthropic-ai/sdk runtime headers bundled by Claude Code.
identity_fingerprint.claude_session_desc: Controls the Claude Code session ID used by headers and metadata.user_id.
identity_fingerprint.claude_device_id_hint: Optional 64-character hex ID. Leave blank to derive a stable value per OAuth account.
identity_fingerprint.claude_custom_headers_hint: Enter a JSON object. Authorization, User-Agent, Anthropic-Beta, X-Stainless-*, and session headers are managed by the system.
identity_fingerprint.claude_preview_desc: After saving and enabling, Anthropic OAuth requests receive these Claude Code identity values.
identity_fingerprint.claude_notice: Claude saves to identity-fingerprint.claude and affects following Anthropic OAuth upstream requests immediately.
identity_fingerprint.gemini_desc: Edit the shared headers written to every gemini-api-key entry in config.yaml.
identity_fingerprint.gemini_headers_hint: Enter a JSON object. These headers are copied into each configured Gemini API key entry.
identity_fingerprint.gemini_preview_desc: Gemini currently supports concrete identity overrides through per-key headers.
identity_fingerprint.gemini_notice: Saving applies this header set to every Gemini API key entry so the runtime can inject it into upstream requests.
identity_fingerprint.gemini_empty_notice: No gemini-api-key entries were found in config.yaml, so there is nothing to update yet.
identity_fingerprint.kimi_desc: Edit the global Kimi CLI identity headers used by Kimi upstream requests.
identity_fingerprint.kimi_user_agent_hint: Value for the User-Agent header sent to Kimi.
identity_fingerprint.kimi_preview_desc: These values map to kimi-header-defaults in config.yaml.
identity_fingerprint.kimi_notice: Kimi saves to kimi-header-defaults and affects following Kimi upstream requests after the config is persisted.

m_providers.title: AI Providers
m_providers.no_match_hint: Try different keywords or clear the search
m_providers.confirm_delete_provider: Delete OpenAI provider "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
m_providers.confirm_delete_generic: Delete this config? This cannot be undone.
m_providers.excluded_placeholder: One model per line; use * to disable all
m_providers.edit_provider_desc: Configure name/baseUrl, API key entries, headers and model aliases; supports auto-fetch via /models.
m_providers.routing_rules_desc: Leave empty for default routing; configure for model aliases, priority, etc.
m_providers.validation_alias_required: Models must have alias (use name => alias)
m_providers.validation_priority_number: Model {{name}}'s priority must be a number

m_api_keys.title: API Keys Management
m_api_keys.no_keys_desc: Create an API Key to start using the proxy service

m_login.auto_connecting_desc: Attempting to connect using locally saved credentials

m_apikey_lookup.title: API Key Lookup
m_apikey_lookup.input_placeholder: Enter API Key to query usage
m_apikey_lookup.no_data: Enter an API Key to query

request_logs.title: Request Logs
request_logs.heading: Request Logs
request_logs.clear_database_logs_confirm: This clears SQLite request-log history and stored message bodies. API keys and other management configuration stay intact. This action cannot be undone.
request_logs.clear_database_logs_success: Cleared {{count}} request logs from the database

ccswitch.import_modal_desc: Choose the client configuration to add to CC Switch.
ccswitch.settings_description: Manage reusable CC Switch import presets for different clients, provider names, models, and channel group scopes.
ccswitch.settings_endpoint_path_placeholder: Leave empty to use the server root
ccswitch.settings_default_model_placeholder: Leave empty to choose from available models
ccswitch.settings_reset: CC Switch import settings restored to defaults
ccswitch.protocol_unavailable: CC Switch is not installed or the protocol handler is unavailable.
ccswitch.import_card_list_desc: Select a CC Switch preset to import
ccswitch.import_no_compatible_configs: No compatible CC Switch configs found. Create one in CC Switch import settings first.
ccswitch.config_table_description: {{count}} saved preset(s) for CC Switch imports.
ccswitch.config_list_empty: No CC Switch configs yet. Create one to reuse provider presets.
ccswitch.config_modal_description: Create a compact CC Switch provider preset from one channel group and its model mapping.
ccswitch.config_full_base_url_hint: Generated live from the current service root {{baseUrl}}. CC Switch imports will use this endpoint after saving.
ccswitch.config_delete_description: Delete preset "{{name}}"? This only removes it from the local config list.
ccswitch.config_model_mapping_hint: Expose the request model name CC Switch should send, then route it to the actual model available in this channel group.
ccswitch.config_claude_model_mapping_hint: Set Claude Code's main, Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus defaults, with editable request names.
ccswitch.config_model_mapping_empty: No models are available for this channel group.
ccswitch.config_model_mapping_select_group_first: Select a channel group to load available models.

api_keys_page.title: API Keys Management
api_keys_page.description: Create and manage API Keys, then bind each key to a reusable permission config.
api_keys_page.create_desc: Fill in details and generate a new API Key (name required)
api_keys_page.edit_desc: Modify name, key, and the reusable permission config
api_keys_page.delete_warning: This cannot be undone. All clients using this Key will lose access.
api_keys_page.system_prompt_hint: Optional. Requests using this API Key will automatically inject this System Prompt.
api_keys_page.delete_logs_option: Also clear historical request records for this API key
api_keys_page.no_keys_desc: Click “Create key” to add your first API key.
api_keys_page.spending_limit_help: Maximum cumulative API key cost in USD. Empty or 0 means unlimited.
api_keys_page.name_required: Please enter an API key name
api_keys_page.deleted_success_with_logs: Deleted successfully and cleared {{count}} historical request records
api_keys_page.form_permission_profile_desc: Permission configs contain limits, channel/model restrictions, and prompt text. Empty uses the unrestricted default.
api_keys_page.form_exact_channels_desc: Prefer channel groups for normal routing. Enabling this adds an extra exact-channel filter and can hide sibling channels in the same pool.
api_keys_page.form_exact_channels_intersection_warning: Channel groups and exact channels are both set. Requests will use the intersection, not the union.
api_keys_page.form_system_prompt_desc: Requests using this API key will automatically inject this system prompt.

api_key_permissions_page.title: API Key Permissions
api_key_permissions_page.description: Manage reusable permission configs. API Keys only need to choose one config.
api_key_permissions_page.empty_desc: Create a config with limits, permissions, and system prompt, then select it in API Keys.
api_key_permissions_page.config_modal_desc: A config includes limits, channel/model permissions, and system prompt. Empty or 0 means unrestricted.
api_key_permissions_page.delete_desc: Delete {{name}}? API Keys using it will keep the copied settings as custom config.

channel_groups_page.title: Channel Groups Management
channel_groups_page.routing_strategy_hint: Applies only among candidate channels inside the same group. Priority is a weight; empty means 1 and 0 excludes the channel.
channel_groups_page.routing_strategy_tooltip: round-robin rotates candidates and keeps prompt cache warm; fill-first keeps using the first available candidate to maximize prompt cache reuse.
channel_groups_page.include_default_group_short: Keep the default group available when no custom group matches.
channel_groups_page.groups_desc: Each group can match by prefix or channel name.
channel_groups_page.routes_desc: Route specific request paths into a target group.
channel_groups_page.routes_modal_desc: Maintain path-to-group mappings in one place.
channel_groups_page.delete_group_desc: Delete channel group {{group}}? This also deletes {{count}} path routes bound to this group and cannot be undone.
channel_groups_page.stale_channels_required_cleanup: {{count}} deleted channels are still referenced. Remove them before saving.
channel_groups_page.group_modal_desc: Name the group, then search and add channels as members.
channel_groups_page.stale_alert_title: This group still references deleted channels
channel_groups_page.stale_alert_message: These channels were removed from auth files or provider configs. Clear the stale references before saving. {{count}} invalid channels remain.
channel_groups_page.stale_toast_message: {{group}} still references {{count}} deleted channels. Open the editor and clean them up.
channel_groups_page.route_modal_desc: Bind a request path to a target group and choose fallback behavior.
channel_groups_page.select_channel_hint: Channels come from AI Providers and Auth Files.
channel_groups_page.allowed_models_hint: The model list is filtered by the selected channels. Leaving it empty allows every model those channels can serve.
channel_groups_page.models_need_channels: Select channels in Basic config first.
channel_groups_page.no_channel_models: No models are available for the selected channels.
channel_groups_page.group_routes_desc: These paths route directly into this group.
channel_groups_page.prefixes_placeholder: One prefix per line or separated by commas
channel_groups_page.channels_placeholder: One channel name per line or separated by commas
channel_groups_page.match_input_hint: Supports comma or newline separated input.
channel_groups_page.route_path_hint: Enter the path after the domain, for example /pro or /openai/pro; you can also paste the full access URL
channel_groups_page.route_path_invalid: Invalid path. Enter the path after the domain, for example /pro or /openai/pro.
channel_groups_page.save_failed: Failed to save channel group configuration
channel_groups_page.load_failed: Failed to load channel group configuration

apikey_lookup.placeholder: Enter API Key to lookup usage
apikey_lookup.title: API key usage
apikey_lookup.daily_usage_desc: Last {{days}} days · request and token trends
apikey_lookup.empty_desc: Enter your API key above to view detailed usage statistics and request logs.

auth_files_page.title: Auth Files
auth_files_page.description: Manage auth file upload/enable/download, OAuth excluded models & model aliases.
auth_files_page.add_oauth_desc: Authorize providers in the browser to generate OAuth auth files, or import iFlow/Vertex credentials.
auth_files_page.no_files_desc: You can import JSON auth files via the Upload button.
auth_files_page.excluded_desc: Maintain disabled models list by provider (one model per line).
auth_files_page.no_excluded_desc: You can add a provider and save the excluded models list.
auth_files_page.alias_no_config: You can add a channel and maintain mapping.
auth_files_page.cannot_edit_desc: Server did not return models or channel unsupported.
auth_files_page.models_hint: Auth file might not support querying models, or server did not return data.

models_page.title: Model Configuration Management
models_page.description: Maintain model IDs, descriptions, status, and pricing stored by the backend database.
models_page.delete_model_desc: Delete {{model}} from the database-backed model configuration list? This action only happens after confirmation.
models_page.delete_selected_models_desc_one: Delete {{count}} selected model from the database-backed model configuration list? This action only happens after confirmation.
models_page.delete_selected_models_desc_other: Delete {{count}} selected models from the database-backed model configuration list? This action only happens after confirmation.
models_page.owner_presets_desc: Maintain owner options and quickly review the models under each owner.
models_page.owner_form_desc: Keep the owner value, display name, and dropdown availability tidy.
models_page.delete_owner_desc: Delete {{owner}} from owner presets? {{count}} models currently use this owner value; the models will stay unchanged.
models_page.owner_remove_disabled: Move or delete this owner's models before removing it
models_page.model_pricing_desc: Manage pricing for all available models. Values are stored in the database for quota calculations.
models_page.model_configs_desc: Models are loaded from database-backed configuration. Edit IDs, descriptions, status, and billing rules here.
models_page.openrouter_sync_load_failed: Failed to load OpenRouter sync state
models_page.openrouter_sync_save_failed: Failed to save OpenRouter sync settings
models_page.config_saved: Model configuration saved to the database
models_page.config_desc: All model fields and prices are persisted by the management API.
models_page.description_placeholder: Describe model purpose, provider, or billing notes

providers.name: Name
providers.openai_desc: API Key / Prefix / Base URL / Excluded Models / Headers / Models
providers.openai_config_desc: Configure name/baseUrl, apiKeyEntries, headers & model aliases; supports /models auto-fetch.
providers.ampcode_desc: Configure upstream URL / API Key, model mapping & force mapping.
providers.vertex_desc: Models must have name=>alias to map downstream model names to Vertex.
providers.claude_desc: Multiple keys, headers, model aliases & /models discovery.
providers.force_mapping_desc: Only allow models in the mapping list.
providers.gemini_desc: Supports baseUrl / proxyUrl / headers / models config.
providers.codex_desc: Supports proxyUrl / custom headers / model aliases / Excluded Models (* to disable all).
providers.opencode_go_desc: Fixed OpenCode Go upstream; paste your OpenCode key and all Go-plan models are routed automatically.
providers.bedrock_desc: Supports AWS Bedrock API key or SigV4 credentials, region, headers, and Claude model aliases.
providers.excluded_placeholder: One model per line; * disables all
providers.no_config_desc: Click Add New to create the first config.
providers.no_openai_desc: Click Add provider to create your first configuration.
providers.import_preview_desc: Review the normalized diff for {{filename}} before replacing the current provider configuration.
providers.import_invalid: The selected file is not a valid provider export JSON.
providers.import_provider_mismatch: This file belongs to a different provider tab.
providers.config_overview_desc: Manage API keys, OpenAI-compatible providers, and Ampcode mappings in one place.
providers.vertex_config_desc: Vertex models must define aliases (name => alias). Use * to disable all models.
providers.bedrock_config_desc: Use a Bedrock API key or AWS SigV4 credentials. Bedrock requests are sent to the selected runtime region.
providers.opencode_go_config_desc: OpenCode Go uses the fixed opencode.ai Go endpoint. No Base URL or model list is needed.
providers.generic_config_desc: Supports excluded models (one per line; * disables all), custom headers, and model settings.
providers.channel_name_hint: Use a recognizable name to distinguish channels.
providers.key_entry_error: At least one API key entry is required
providers.disable_hint: Disabling writes * into excluded models. You can still edit the list manually below.
providers.api_key_hint: Paste the key only; extra text may break source tracking consistency.
providers.bedrock_auth_mode_hint: API key uses Bearer authentication; SigV4 signs requests with AWS credentials.
providers.bedrock_api_key_hint: Paste the Bedrock API key issued by AWS; leave SigV4 fields empty in this mode.
providers.bedrock_sigv4_hint: Session token is only needed for temporary AWS credentials.
providers.bedrock_sigv4_error: AWS access key ID and secret access key cannot be empty
providers.bedrock_region_hint: Used to build the Bedrock Runtime endpoint, for example us-east-1.
providers.bedrock_force_global_hint: Send model IDs without adding a regional prefix.
providers.prefix_hint: Prefixes help with routing and stats matching across multiple key entries.
providers.connection_proxy_hint: Base URL switches the upstream; proxy pool binding is preferred, and Proxy URL remains a per-key fallback.
providers.opencode_go_connection_hint: The upstream is fixed to OpenCode Go. Proxy pool binding is optional for egress control.
providers.opencode_go_fixed_endpoint_hint: Chat, Anthropic Messages, and model discovery use official OpenCode Go endpoints. You only need to provide your OpenCode key.
providers.proxy_pool_hint: Select a managed proxy when this key must use a fixed egress IP. Proxy URL below is used as fallback.
providers.headers_common_hint: Common examples: x-api-key, anthropic-version, or custom auth headers.
providers.vertex_alias_hint: Vertex requires downstream model names to map to Vertex-recognized aliases.
providers.models_default_hint: Leave empty to use default routing; fill in values to enable aliases, priorities, and more.
providers.model_group_hint: Select a model category and click Load to quickly import common models from that vendor. Existing models will not be duplicated.
providers.models_hint: Hint: One model per line; alias rewrites downstream model name. Higher priority number takes precedence.
providers.opencode_go_models_hint: Models are loaded from the official OpenCode Go /models endpoint. Checked models are allowed; unchecked models are written to the blocked list.
providers.fill_base_url_first: Please fill in the Base URL first
providers.confirm_delete_openai: Delete OpenAI-compatible provider "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
providers.confirm_delete_config: Delete this configuration? This cannot be undone.
providers.confirm_delete_generic: Are you sure you want to delete this item?
providers.access_none: No API keys can reach this channel
providers.access_exact_overrides: {{count}} API keys use exact channel overrides
providers.anthropic_processing_hint: When enabled, applies Anthropic-specific processing (cloaking, cache control, tool prefix). Disable for third-party Claude-compatible APIs (e.g. Kimi) to improve performance.

logs_page.no_error_desc: Current server has no downloadable Error Log files.
logs_page.error_fetch_desc: Fetch Error Log files from server and support downloading by Request ID.
logs_page.no_logs_desc: You can click Refresh or enable Auto Refresh to pull latest logs.
logs_page.status_summary: Auto Refresh: {{autoRefresh}} · Hide management traffic: {{hideManagement}} · Raw logs: {{rawLogs}}
logs_page.request_id_download_desc: Input Request ID (8 chars) to download corresponding log.
logs_page.error_log_list_desc: List by file name, click Download to obtain file.
logs_page.confirm_clear_logs: Are you sure you want to clear server logs? This operation is irreversible.
logs_page.failed_fetch_error_list: Failed to fetch error log list

oauth.vertex_desc: Upload Vertex credentials file to generate auth file.
oauth.callback_placeholder: Paste the full callback URL from browser
oauth.callback_browser_address_hint: After authorizing in the browser, the browser address bar contains the callback URL. Copy the full URL and submit it below.
oauth.authorization_proxy_hint: Optional. Leave empty to use the deployed server's local network egress.
oauth.iflow_hint_default: Use the iFlow Cookie for a one-time auth import.

codex.title: Codex OAuth
codex.hint: Start the authorization flow and let the server save the auth file automatically.

anthropic.title: Anthropic OAuth
anthropic.hint: Use this for Claude / Anthropic OAuth login.

antigravity.title: Antigravity OAuth
antigravity.hint: Use this for Antigravity quota and capability OAuth login.

gemini_cli.title: Gemini CLI OAuth
gemini_cli.hint: Supports an optional Project ID; the server auto-selects one when empty.

kimi.title: Kimi OAuth
kimi.hint: If supported by the server, the auth file is saved automatically.

qwen.title: Qwen OAuth
qwen.hint: If supported by the server, the auth file is saved automatically.

log_content.title: View request input and model output

error_detail.historical_missing: This historical request did not record any upstream error response
error_detail.no_content: This historical log did not record any upstream error response, so the original upstream failure cannot be reconstructed. New failed logs will store the actual error details.

visual_config.tls_desc: Enable TLS and configure certificate paths.
visual_config.cors_desc: Edit `cors-allow-origins` as one origin per line.
visual_config.cors_origins_hint: Only needed for cross-origin browser callers. Same-host origins are already allowed.
visual_config.cors_default_desc: Browser extensions using `chrome-extension://<extension-id>` are allowed by default. Add other origins here when a browser app or local dev tool still gets blocked.
visual_config.payload_default_desc: After matching models, append/override parameters in the request payload (corresponds to `payload.default`).
visual_config.payload_override_desc: After matching models, override parameters in the request payload (corresponds to `payload.override`).
visual_config.payload_filter_desc: After matching models, remove specified parameter paths from the request payload (corresponds to `payload.filter`).
visual_config.prefer_ipv4_desc: Force all outbound upstream connections over IPv4 only. Useful when IPv6 is via tunnel and has poor quality.
visual_config.include_default_group_desc: Keep unprefixed channels addressable as the implicit `default` group.
visual_config.routing_groups_desc: Define named channel groups for API key permissions and grouped URLs.
visual_config.group_name_hint: Case-insensitive group name used by routing and API keys.
visual_config.group_priority_hint: Higher values are preferred first; leave empty for 0.
visual_config.group_description_hint: Optional note shown in management UI.
visual_config.group_prefixes_hint: One prefix per line or comma-separated.
visual_config.group_channels_hint: Match exact channel display names, one per line or comma-separated.
visual_config.no_channel_groups: No channel groups. Prefixes still create implicit groups automatically.
visual_config.path_routes_desc: Map URL namespaces such as `/pro` to channel groups.
visual_config.path_route_fallback_hint: Fallback only relaxes path scope, never API key permissions.
visual_config.strip_prefix_desc: Forward `/pro/v1/...` as `/v1/...` after matching the route.
visual_config.no_path_routes: No explicit path routes. Known group names still work as implicit URLs.
visual_config.api_key_charset_err: API Key only allows visible ASCII characters
visual_config.api_migrated: API Keys have been migrated to the dedicated management page.
visual_config.kimi_headers_desc: Configure headers used when calling Kimi API, affecting how requests appear in Kimi console.
visual_config.kimi_headers_note: Note: Leave empty to use Kimi CLI defaults. Changes take effect on next request after saving.

config_page.runtime_desc: These configs are written to server via management API (complements config.yaml).
config_page.debug_desc: Enable verbose debug logs (recommended for temporary use).
config_page.request_logs_desc: Record request logs for querying and troubleshooting.
config_page.log_to_file_desc: Write logs to file (for downloading error logs).
config_page.auto_update_desc: Prompt when a newer Docker image is available.
config_page.auto_update_channel_desc: Choose whether update checks and Docker pulls use the main or dev branch image. Default is main.
config_page.auto_update_docker_image_desc: Use a custom image mirror or private registry when this environment cannot access GHCR/Docker Hub. Tags still follow the selected branch: latest for main, dev for dev.
config_page.auto_update_docker_image_warning: Custom images can break updates or crash the service if the registry image is stale, incompatible, tampered with, or missing the expected tags. Only use a mirror you trust and can recover from.
config_page.logs_routing_desc: Log size limit and routing strategy.
config_page.search_hint: Supports search, prev/next match & keyboard shortcuts.
config_page.empty_desc: Server may not have config.yaml or API returned empty.
config_page.discard_desc: You have unsaved changes. Reloading will discard them. Continue?
config_page.visual_desc: Scroll to edit; saves to server.
config_page.channel_groups_moved: Channel groups and path routes are now managed from the dedicated page.
config_page.retry_non_negative: Retry count must be a non-negative number
config_page.log_size_non_negative: Log size limit must be a non-negative number

auto_update.title: New Version Found
auto_update.description: Review the release notes before updating the Docker deployment.
auto_update.up_to_date_description: The current deployment is already updated to the latest version for the selected channel.
auto_update.toast_message: A new version is available: {{version}}. Update now?
auto_update.system_description: Check Docker image updates, review release notes, and apply updates when ready.
auto_update.system_idle: Click Check Docker Update to load the latest version details.
auto_update.no_release_notes: No release notes were provided for this update.
auto_update.updating_description: The updater is running now. Follow the version transition and live Docker logs below.
auto_update.completed_description: The updater finished successfully. Review the version transition and Docker logs below.
auto_update.progress_logs_empty: Waiting for the updater to emit Docker logs…
auto_update.updater_unavailable: The updater sidecar is not reachable. Docker deployments installed with the latest compose file can update from here.
auto_update.failed_description: The updater reported a failure. Review the Docker logs below.
auto_update.timeout: Update was triggered, but the server heartbeat did not recover in time. Please check Docker logs.
auto_update.version_mismatch: Update was triggered, but the running version is still not {{version}}. Please check the updater and Docker Compose logs.
